zh.Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland: 蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith於1395年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯時，他的父親Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith25歲，母親Princess Elizabeth Stewart23歲。他於1417年與Lady Elizabeth Gifford在蘇格蘭度福利爾莫頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1457年3月14日在他的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯。

zh-hans.Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland: 苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith于1395年出生在苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯时，他的父亲Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith25岁，母亲Princess Elizabeth Stewart23岁。他于1417年与Lady Elizabeth Gifford在苏格兰度福利爾莫頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1457年3月14日在他的家乡享年62岁，埋葬在苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith was born in 1395, in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, his father, Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith, was 25 and his mother, Princess Elizabeth Stewart, was 23. He married Lady Elizabeth Gifford in 1417, in Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 14 March 1457, in his hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith est né en 1395, à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, son père, Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Princess Elizabeth Stewart, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth Gifford en 1417, à Morton, Dumfriesshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 14 mars 1457, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith 1395 in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith 25 und seine Mutter Princess Elizabeth Stewart 23. Er hat 1417 in Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland Lady Elizabeth Gifford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 14. März 1457 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith è nato il nel 1395 nel Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Princess Elizabeth Stewart, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Lady Elizabeth Gifford nel 1417, nel Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 14 marzo 1457, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeithが1395年にスコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスで生まれた時，父Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeithは25歳，母Princess Elizabeth Stewartは23歳であった。1417年にスコットランドダムフリーシュアイアモートンでLady Elizabeth Giffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1457年3月14日に故郷で62歳で死去し，スコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith(이)가 1395년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Princess Elizabeth Stewart(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1417년 스컷랜드 드프리실르 모르턴에서 Lady Elizabeth Gifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1457년 3월 14일 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith nasceu em 1395, em Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, seu pai, Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Princess Elizabeth Stewart, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Elizabeth Gifford em 1417, em Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de março de 1457, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith родился в 1395 г. в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Princess Elizabeth Stewart, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth Gifford в 1417 г. в местности мортон, думфриессхир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 14 марта 1457 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James II Douglas, Lord of Dalkeith nació en 1395, en Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, su papá, Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Princess Elizabeth Stewart, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Lady Elizabeth Gifford en 1417, en Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 14 de marzo de 1457, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland.
